verses.text: The LORD: what man kills ox, lamb, or goat in or out of camp, and brings it not to the tabernacle door — blood imputed to him; cut off. Bring sacrifices from the field; priest sprinkles blood, burns fat — sweet savor. No more sacrifices to demons after whom they have gone a-whoring. A statute forever. Guidance: All worship comes to one address. Israel had been killing animals on hillsides for the goat-demons; God closes every back-alley altar. Worship belongs at His door or nowhere.

Bring sacrifices from the field; priest sprinkles blood, burns fat — sweet savor. No more sacrifices to demons after whom they have gone a-whoring. A statute forever. Guidance: All worship comes to one address. Israel had been killing animals on hillsides for the goat-demons; God closes every back-alley altar. Worship belongs at His door or nowhere.

Any man of Israel, or of the strangers among them, that offers burnt offering or sacrifice and brings it not to the tabernacle door to offer to the LORD — that man shall be cut off from his people. Guidance: The same rule extends to foreigners living among Israel. God's house is the only address for sacrifice, no matter who you are. Universality cuts both ways: open to all, narrow as a door.

Any man of Israel, or of the strangers among them, that offers burnt offering or sacrifice and brings it not to the tabernacle door to offer to the LORD — that man shall be cut off from his people. Guidance: The same rule extends to foreigners living among Israel. God's house is the only address for sacrifice, no matter who you are. Universality cuts both ways: open to all, narrow as a door.

If any man eat blood — I will set My face against him, cut off. The life of the flesh is in the blood; I have given it on the altar to atone; the blood atones by the life. No soul shall eat blood, neither stranger. Guidance: The verse Hebrews 9:22 reaches back to: without shedding of blood is no remission. Blood carries life; God reserves life-fluid for His altar. To eat it is to take what belongs to atonement and make it food.

No soul shall eat blood, neither stranger. Guidance: The verse Hebrews 9:22 reaches back to: without shedding of blood is no remission. Blood carries life; God reserves life-fluid for His altar. Whatever man of Israel or strangers hunts a beast or fowl that may be eaten — pour out the blood, cover with dust. The life of all flesh is the blood; whoever eats it shall be cut off. Guidance: Even game from a hunt has the rule. Pour the blood out, cover it with dust — give it back to the ground. Hunters and farmers alike honor the same boundary: blood goes to God, never to the table.

Whatever man of Israel or strangers hunts a beast or fowl that may be eaten — pour out the blood, cover with dust. The life of all flesh is the blood; whoever eats it shall be cut off. Guidance: Even game from a hunt has the rule. Pour the blood out, cover it with dust — give it back to the ground. Hunters and farmers alike honor the same boundary: blood goes to God, never to the table.

The soul that eats what dies of itself or is torn — wash clothes, bathe in water, unclean till evening; then clean. If not washed or bathed — bear his iniquity. Guidance: Eating something that died on its own brings impurity, but not unforgivable sin — washing restores you. The danger comes from refusing the washing. Even minor uncleanness needs honest acknowledgment, never a shrug.
